channel.title: Comments on: Ska Brewing: Modus Hoperandi, Ep. 124
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Video BrewMaster
item.dc:creator: Video BrewMaster
item.description:
Ska is doing really great beers, and Modus Hoperandi is a top tier IPA for sure! Joseph, we have reviewed at least one rye beer, you can find it here
http://beeramerica.tv/episode-27-terra-ryezd-black-rye-lager/
Thanks for the comments everyone!!
item.content:encoded:
Ska is doing really great beers, and Modus Hoperandi is a top tier IPA for sure! Joseph, we have reviewed at least one rye beer, you can find it here

http://beeramerica.tv/episode-27-terra-ryezd-black-rye-lager/

Thanks for the comments everyone!!

item.title: By: Joseph Allen
item.dc:creator: Joseph Allen
item.description: Hello guys I watch your show. I love beer, my favorite styles are Belgium tripels, Imperial IPA, and I love Guinness. I ve had many of the beers you've reviewed and their are many I have, not like ska. I live in the city of Buffalo, NY, and have great selection here. I wish the beers like ska or mother earth or many other more western beers would come to me. I'am glad to know there is so much still to try out and I'm gonna keep watching. Will review a rye beer? I homebrew, rye beer is the next beer to make for us. __Joseph
item.content:encoded: Hello guys I watch your show. I love beer, my favorite styles are Belgium tripels, Imperial IPA, and I love Guinness. I ve had many of the beers you’ve reviewed and their are many I have, not like ska. I live in the city of Buffalo, NY, and have great selection here. I wish the beers like ska or mother earth or many other more western beers would come to me. I’am glad to know there is so much still to try out and I’m gonna keep watching. Will review a rye beer? I homebrew, rye beer is the next beer to make for us. __Joseph

item.title: By: Gary Saunders
item.dc:creator: Gary Saunders
item.description: Great episode guys. Now I'm thirsty. Thanks. I look forward to the next show.
item.content:encoded: Great episode guys. Now I’m thirsty. Thanks. I look forward to the next show.

item.title: By: Tim McCarty
item.dc:creator: Tim McCarty
item.description: Hey Guys, Great show! I need to get it direct in the house more often, but I just get too jealous because I live in Okinawa, Japan. I am brewing my own beer here, but I do try AS MUCH AS I CAN when I get stateside. I really enjoy your reviews, they help me pick some awesome brews when I get back! On t=your video for Ska Brewery, I am assuming you read the list on the can and it had HHH. I believe it stands for Hash House Harriers. They are a "Drinking club with a running problem." They do fun runs around an area, chasing "Hares" who make a trail that you have to follow while trying to catch them. When you finish, you drink some beer. This is a great ending to a nice run! Thanks for your show, I learn a lot and get motivated to make, buy, and dring some great brews! Cheers! Tim
item.content:encoded:
Hey Guys,
Great show! I need to get it direct in the house more often, but I just get too jealous because I live in Okinawa, Japan. I am brewing my own beer here, but I do try AS MUCH AS I CAN when I get stateside. I really enjoy your reviews, they help me pick some awesome brews when I get back! On t=your video for Ska Brewery, I am assuming you read the list on the can and it had HHH. I believe it stands for Hash House Harriers. They are a “Drinking club with a running problem.” They do fun runs around an area, chasing “Hares” who make a trail that you have to follow while trying to catch them. When you finish, you drink some beer. This is a great ending to a nice run! Thanks for your show, I learn a lot and get motivated to make, buy, and dring some great brews! Cheers! Tim

item.title: By: Black Hole Brew 42
item.dc:creator: Black Hole Brew 42
item.description:
Love Modus...
"Lip Up Fatty" is a reference to the British ska band Bad Manners, Fronted by the great Buster Bloodvessel. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F6fQnTyEniM
Durango is a sweet town, SW Colorado. Four breweries in a town of under 20,000 people; Ska, Carver, Durango, and Steamworks.
Keep up the good work! If you can find any Hair of the Dog brewing from Portland, OR, get it... The brewer is an ex-chef, could be interesting to show the elements of brewing similar to cooking as a topic, how this is truly influencing the craft brewing community. Cheers!
item.content:encoded:
Love Modus…

“Lip Up Fatty” is a reference to the British ska band Bad Manners, Fronted by the great Buster Bloodvessel.
http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F6fQnTyEniM

Durango is a sweet town, SW Colorado. Four breweries in a town of under 20,000 people; Ska, Carver, Durango, and Steamworks.

Keep up the good work! If you can find any Hair of the Dog brewing from Portland, OR, get it… The brewer is an ex-chef, could be interesting to show the elements of brewing similar to cooking as a topic, how this is truly influencing the craft brewing community. Cheers!

item.title: By: Marco
item.dc:creator: Marco
item.description:
Nice show! I enjoyed an Sierra Nevada Torpedo clone watching this vid. I put that on my list for "beers to look out for" at the Beverage Warehouse in Winooski VT. Here in Canada, our Gov seems to think THEY know what a good beer is. Imagine that!
Cheers guys and keep em coming!
Marco
item.content:encoded:
Nice show! I enjoyed an Sierra Nevada Torpedo clone watching this vid.
I put that on my list for “beers to look out for” at the Beverage Warehouse in Winooski VT.
Here in Canada, our Gov seems to think THEY know what a good beer is. Imagine that!

Cheers guys and keep em coming!

Marco
